Modal.notDesignedYetBody: This round ships the shared visual grammar plus {readyCount, plural, one {# fully-realized agent} other {# fully-realized agents}} ({readyNames}) end to end. The remaining {remainingCount} — including {name} — follow incrementally, each with its own hand-drawn pose and color, not a recolored template.
Modal.unavailableCopy: We couldn't load {name}'s prompt from the Forge repository at build time. You can still view the source file directly on <link>GitHub</link>.

Install.heading: One prompt now. All the specialists, permanently.
Install.subhead: Copying a prompt above works on its own — paste it, done, nothing installed. Installing Forge adds the other half: an orchestrator that pulls in the right specialists on its own, project after project, instead of you hunting for one prompt at a time.
Install.step1Legend: Symlinks every agent into ~/.claude/agents/ — Claude Code picks them up right away, no restart.
Install.step2Caption: In Claude Code, in your own project folder:
Install.examplePhrase: "I have an idea for an app that helps small gyms schedule classes and manage member check-ins."
Install.step2Legend: Any plain description works. Already mid-project? Say "let's loop in the team" instead — the orchestrator switches to triage mode.
Install.step3Title: The orchestrator takes it from there
Install.step3Caption: No command to run — this is what happens automatically:
Install.step3Body: It scopes the idea, delegates to whichever specialists apply — design, backend, mobile, whichever the project actually needs — and synthesizes everything back into one plan, flagging any trade-off a specialist raised along the way.
Install.readmeLinkPrefix: Prerequisites, customizing the roster for your own stack, and the full architecture doc all live in the repo.
Install.readmeLinkCta: See the full walkthrough in the README →

Footer.text: Forge Skill Library — prompts sourced from the public Forge repository.
